title: Bringing family if you have an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Bringing family if you have an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV)
If you have an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), you may be able to support visas for your partner and dependent children, depending on your job and how much you earn.

## Visas you can support for your family
If you have an Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV), you may be able to support:
- a visitor visa for your partner
- a work visa for your partner
- visitor visas for your dependent children
- student visas for your dependent children.
Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa
Partner of a Worker Work Visa
Dependent Child Student Visa
Child of a Worker Visitor Visa
The type of visa you can support for your partner and dependent children will depend on:
- the skill level of your occupation
- if your job is on the list of National Occupation List (NOL) occupations we can recognise for an AEWV
- if your job is on the list of approved transport jobs for the Transport Work to Residence Visa
- if your job is on the list of approved care workforce jobs for the Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa
- if your job is on the Green List and if you meet the job's Green List requirements
- if you supported a visa for your family before 26 June 2024.
ANZSCO grades jobs according to skill level. ANZSCO skill levels range from 1 to 5, with 1 being the most skilled and 5 being less skilled.
Find your ANZSCO skill level
NOL is replacing ANZSCO. Some NOL occupations can now be used for an AEWV.
National Occupation List occupations used for an AEWV

## When you can support visas for your family
If you have an AEWV, your job must meet certain requirements before you can support a family member's visa.
Check the scenario that applies to you and your job. If you and your partner both have an AEWV, you can combine your incomes to meet the minimum income threshold requirement to support dependent children.
If more than 1 scenario applies, use the scenario that is most favourable to your situation.
For example, if you work in an ANZSCO level 5 job, and your job is on the Green List, you may be able to support your family. Check the scenario 'If your job is on the Green List' to find out what type of visa you may be able to support.
- If you earn at least NZD $52.50 an hour (1.5 times the ), you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $52.50 an hour (1.5 times the ), you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
- This includes if your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 but is recognised at a higher level under ANZSCO for an AEWV. If you earn less than NZD $28.00 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn NZD $28.00 or more an hour (80% of the ), you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support a Dependent Child Student Visa or a Child of Worker Visitor Visa if, on or before 9 March 2025: your dependent child had been approved for a visa, or had applied for a visa based on their relationship to you, and you had an AEWV or had applied for an AEWV that was later approved.
This includes if your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 but is recognised at a higher level under ANZSCO for an AEWV.
If you earn less than NZD $28.00 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn NZD $28.00 or more an hour (80% of the ), you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support a Dependent Child Student Visa or a Child of Worker Visitor Visa if, on or before 9 March 2025:
- your dependent child had been approved for a visa, or had applied for a visa based on their relationship to you, and
- you had an AEWV or had applied for an AEWV that was later approved.
- If you earn less than NZD $28.00 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn NZD $28.00 or more an hour (80% of the median wage ), you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa. National Occupation List occupations used for an AEWV or SPWV
If you earn less than NZD $28.00 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn NZD $28.00 or more an hour (80% of the median wage ), you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
National Occupation List occupations used for an AEWV or SPWV
- If your job is on the Green List and you meet the job's Green List requirements, you can support: a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa. If your job is on the Green List, you meet the job’s Green List requirements, and you are paid at least NZD $35.00 an hour you can also support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa. Green List roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand
If your job is on the Green List and you meet the job's Green List requirements, you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
If your job is on the Green List, you meet the job’s Green List requirements, and you are paid at least NZD $35.00 an hour you can also support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa.
Green List roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand
- Check if you can support visas for your family while you gain work experience on an AEWV to be able to apply for a Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa. Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 Check the section on this page for people with ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 jobs. If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 If your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 If you earn at least NZD $28.25 an hour, you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support a Dependent Child Student Visa or a Child of Worker Visitor Visa if, on or before 9 March 2025: your dependent child had been approved for a visa, or had applied for a visa based on their relationship to you, and you had an AEWV or had applied for an AEWV that was later approved.
Check if you can support visas for your family while you gain work experience on an AEWV to be able to apply for a Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa.
Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa

#### If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3
Check the section on this page for people with ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 jobs.
If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3

#### If your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5
If you earn at least NZD $28.25 an hour, you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year, you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support a Dependent Child Student Visa or a Child of Worker Visitor Visa if, on or before 9 March 2025:
- your dependent child had been approved for a visa, or had applied for a visa based on their relationship to you, and
- you had an AEWV or had applied for an AEWV that was later approved.
- Check if you can support visas for your family while you gain work experience on an AEWV to be able to apply for a Transport Work to Residence Visa. Transport Work to Residence Visa If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 Check the section on this page for people with ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 jobs. If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 If your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5 If you meet our 'acceptable employment' requirements you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. To meet our acceptable employment requirements, your job must: pay at least NZD $35.00 an hour be with an be (at least 30 hours each week) be permanent, or a fixed-term job that will last at least 12 months from when you make your application, and be a genuine job or job offer, for example you did not pay the employer to get the job offer. If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year and you meet our acceptable employment requirements , you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
Check if you can support visas for your family while you gain work experience on an AEWV to be able to apply for a Transport Work to Residence Visa.
Transport Work to Residence Visa

#### If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3
Check the section on this page for people with ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3 jobs.
If your job is ANZSCO skill level 1, 2 or 3

#### If your job is ANZSCO skill level 4 or 5
If you meet our 'acceptable employment' requirements you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
To meet our acceptable employment requirements, your job must:
- pay at least NZD $35.00 an hour
- be (at least 30 hours each week)
- be permanent, or a fixed-term job that will last at least 12 months from when you make your application, and
- be a genuine job or job offer, for example you did not pay the employer to get the job offer.
If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year and you meet our acceptable employment requirements , you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
- You can support a visa for your family, if your job: was included on the list of approved transport roles before 7 April 2024 and you meet the requirements to count work experience from the job meets our 'acceptable employment' requirements. Care workforce and transport sector pathway to residence To meet our 'acceptable employment' requirements, your job must: pay at least NZD $28.00 per hour, or the wage requirement specified in the sector agreement, whichever is higher be with an be (at least 30 hours each week) be permanent, or a fixed-term job that will last at least 12 months from when you make your application, and be a genuine job or job offer, for example you did not pay the employer to get the job offer. If you meet these requirements you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year and you meet our acceptable employment requirements, you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
You can support a visa for your family, if your job:
- was included on the list of approved transport roles before 7 April 2024 and you meet the requirements to count work experience from the job
- meets our 'acceptable employment' requirements.
Care workforce and transport sector pathway to residence
To meet our 'acceptable employment' requirements, your job must:
- pay at least NZD $28.00 per hour, or the wage requirement specified in the sector agreement, whichever is higher
- be (at least 30 hours each week)
- be permanent, or a fixed-term job that will last at least 12 months from when you make your application, and
- be a genuine job or job offer, for example you did not pay the employer to get the job offer.
If you meet these requirements you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $58,240 a year and you meet our acceptable employment requirements, you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
- You can continue to support a visa for your partner and dependent children. If your partner or child needs to apply for a new visa, you must meet 1 of the current requirements to support a new visa. If your partner was given a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions We may have given your partner a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions before 2 December 2024. A work visa with conditions means your partner must meet some employment rules. They must: work for an be paid at least the wage rate in place for their job, unless they are in a job covered by an uncapped sector agreement. Your partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their visa if you earn at least NZD $28.00 an hour. Check or change your work visa conditions Note If your partner has a Partner of a Worker Work Visa application in progress on or after 2 December 2024, their application will be assessed based on the current requirements.
You can continue to support a visa for your partner and dependent children.
If your partner or child needs to apply for a new visa, you must meet 1 of the current requirements to support a new visa.

#### If your partner was given a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions
We may have given your partner a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions before 2 December 2024. A work visa with conditions means your partner must meet some employment rules.
- be paid at least the wage rate in place for their job, unless they are in a job covered by an uncapped sector agreement.
Your partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their visa if you earn at least NZD $28.00 an hour.
Check or change your work visa conditions
If your partner has a Partner of a Worker Work Visa application in progress on or after 2 December 2024, their application will be assessed based on the current requirements.
- You can continue to support a visa for your partner and dependent children. If your partner or dependent child needs to apply for a new visa You can support a new application for your partner or dependent child. The type of visa you can support depends on how much you earn. It does not depend on the ANZSCO skill level of your job. If you earn less than NZD $26.85 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn NZD $26.85 or more an hour (80% of the median wage), you can support: a Partner of a Worker Work Visa a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa. If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support: a Dependent Child Student Visa a Child of Worker Visitor Visa. Example Rachel has an AEWV as a Transport Conductor (ANZSCO skill level 5). She earns NZD $35 an hour and supported a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with conditions for her partner in March 2024. She can continue to support her partner's visa. Her partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their Partner of a Worker Work Visa. Lou had an Essential Skills Work Visa as a Cook (ANZSCO skill level 4) and earns NZD $29.66 an hour. He has supported visas for his partner and child since August 2022. Lou applied for and was given an AEWV after 10 December 2024. Because Lou earns more than NZD $26.85 an hour, he can continue to support a Partner of a Worker Work Visa for his partner and a Dependent Child Student Visa for his child. His partner is eligible for a work visa with no conditions. If your partner was given a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions We may have given your partner a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions before 26 June 2024. A work visa with conditions means your partner needs to meet some employment rules. They must: work for an accredited employer be paid at least the wage rate in place for their job, unless they are in a job covered by an uncapped sector agreement. Your partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their visa if you earn at least NZD $26.85 an hour. Check or change your work visa conditions
You can continue to support a visa for your partner and dependent children.

#### If your partner or dependent child needs to apply for a new visa
You can support a new application for your partner or dependent child. The type of visa you can support depends on how much you earn. It does not depend on the ANZSCO skill level of your job.
If you earn less than NZD $26.85 an hour, you can only support a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn NZD $26.85 or more an hour (80% of the median wage), you can support:
- a Partner of a Worker Work Visa
- a Partner of a Worker Visitor Visa.
If you earn at least NZD $43,322.76 a year, you can support:
- a Dependent Child Student Visa
- a Child of Worker Visitor Visa.
Rachel has an AEWV as a Transport Conductor (ANZSCO skill level 5). She earns NZD $35 an hour and supported a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with conditions for her partner in March 2024. She can continue to support her partner's visa. Her partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their Partner of a Worker Work Visa.
Lou had an Essential Skills Work Visa as a Cook (ANZSCO skill level 4) and earns NZD $29.66 an hour. He has supported visas for his partner and child since August 2022. Lou applied for and was given an AEWV after 10 December 2024. Because Lou earns more than NZD $26.85 an hour, he can continue to support a Partner of a Worker Work Visa for his partner and a Dependent Child Student Visa for his child. His partner is eligible for a work visa with no conditions.

#### If your partner was given a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions
We may have given your partner a Partner of a Worker Work Visa with work conditions before 26 June 2024. A work visa with conditions means your partner needs to meet some employment rules.
- work for an accredited employer
- be paid at least the wage rate in place for their job, unless they are in a job covered by an uncapped sector agreement.
Your partner can apply to remove the work conditions from their visa if you earn at least NZD $26.85 an hour.
Check or change your work visa conditions
If you do not meet 1 of these scenarios, you cannot support a visa for your partner and children. They may be able to apply for a visa in their own right.
Find and choose a visa
